content:
# 2 Corinthians Chapter 4 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2CO_004_page_001.jpg

### The Light of the Gospel

1. Therefore, since God in his mercy has given us this new way, we never give up.

2. We reject all shameful deeds and underhanded methods. We don't try to trick anyone or distort the word of God. We tell the truth before God, and all who are honest know this.

3. If the Good News we preach is hidden behind a veil, it is hidden only from people who are perishing.

4. Satan, who is the god of this world, has blinded the minds of those who don't believe. They are unable to see the glorious light of the Good News. They don't understand this message about the glory of Christ, who is the exact likeness of God.

5. You see, we don't go around preaching about ourselves. We preach that Jesus Christ is Lord, and we ourselves are your servants for Jesus' sake.

6. For God, who said, "Let there be light in the darkness," has made this light shine in our hearts so we could know the glory of God that is seen in the face of Jesus Christ.

### Treasure in Fragile Clay Jars

7. We now have this light shining in our hearts, but we ourselves are like fragile clay jars containing this great treasure. This makes it clear that our great power is from God, not from ourselves.

8. We are pressed on every side by troubles, but we are not crushed. We are perplexed, but not driven to despair.

9. We are hunted down, but never abandoned by God. We get knocked down, but we are not destroyed.

10. Through suffering, our bodies continue to share in the death of Jesus so that the life of Jesus may also be seen in our bodies.

11. Yes, we live under constant danger of death because we serve Jesus, so that the life of Jesus will be evident in our dying bodies.

12. So we live in the face of death, but this has resulted in eternal life for you.

13. But we continue to preach because we have the same kind of faith the psalmist had when he said, "I believed in God, so I spoke."

14. We know that God, who raised the Lord Jesus, will also raise us with Jesus and present us to himself together with you.

15. All of this is for your benefit. And as God's grace reaches more and more people, there will be great thanksgiving, and God will receive more and more glory.

### Eternal Glory Outweighs Suffering

16. That is why we never give up. Though our bodies are dying, our spirits are being renewed every day.

17. For our present troubles are small and won't last very long. Yet they produce for us a glory that vastly outweighs them and will last forever!

18. So we don't look at the troubles we can see now; rather, we fix our gaze on things that cannot be seen. For the things we see now will soon be gone, but the things we cannot see will last forever.

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 4 — "The god of this world":** This is the only place in the New Testament where Satan is called "god." Paul is not saying Satan is divine — he's saying Satan functions as the de facto ruler of this present darkness. He blinds minds to keep people from seeing what Paul sees: that Jesus Christ is the visible image of the invisible God. The gospel is not hidden because it's obscure — it's hidden because someone is actively hiding it.

**Verse 6 — Creation and the gospel:** Paul connects Genesis 1:3 ("Let there be light") directly to conversion. The same God who spoke light into being at creation now shines light into human hearts. This means becoming a Christian is nothing less than a new creation — God speaks, and light appears where there was only darkness.

**Verse 7 — "Clay jars":** This is the thesis statement of 2 Corinthians. The treasure (the gospel, the glory of God) is contained in the cheapest possible container (fragile, replaceable clay pots). Why? So that everyone knows the power comes from God, not from the messenger. Paul's weakness, his suffering, his near-death experiences — these are not obstacles to his ministry. They ARE the ministry. They prove the power is God's.

**Verses 8-9 — The four contrasts:** Pressed but not crushed. Perplexed but not despairing. Hunted but not abandoned. Knocked down but not destroyed. Each pair describes the same pattern: real suffering but divine preservation. Paul is not denying his pain — he's saying something survives in him that his circumstances cannot destroy.

**Verse 17 — "A glory that vastly outweighs them":** In Hebrew, "glory" (kavod) comes from the word for "heavy" (kaved). Paul may be playing on this: our "light" affliction produces "heavy" glory. The affliction is momentary; the glory is eternal. The math of heaven is different from the math of earth.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
